creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for brand voice in AI systems: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach ensures brand voice consistency across interactions and prevents prompt degradation over extended conversations.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts thousands of personal notes and documents into queryable memory for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, index, wiki) with a file-based deep research algorithm that avoids traditional vector or graph databases, enabling efficient context loading for language models.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa presents experience building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The session covers core components including agent loops, A2A protocol, Effect-TS implementation, evals and feedback systems, long context handling, monitoring, tool building, and enterprise patterns using Claude and Cursor.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context awareness and memory, using the metaphor of a brilliant engineer who can only see fragments of code and forgets between interactions. He positions this as a fundamental problem to solve in agent design.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model comprises only 10% of results while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the other 90%. The video breaks down the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and emphasizes context engineering and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a decentralized memory layer that persists context across multiple AI agents and tools. It stores encrypted, user-owned memories that agents from different providers can access and recall, enabling continuity across Claude, Codex, Pi, and other AI systems.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The video reviews it as impressive for its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Advanced AI engineers use automated agent loops that prompt other agents rather than manually prompting themselves. Cole Medin demonstrates a TypeScript-based system where an orchestrator agent spawns parallel worker agents, each run is logged to a database, and the entire pipeline is visualized in a dashboard. This approach addresses reliability and cost concerns while moving away from repeatedly prompting frontier models directly.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason announces a new secret project from the OpenClaw creator involving Crabbox and codebase harness tools. A GitHub repository with setup skills is provided alongside links to AI Builder Club and Superdesign platform.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is an approach for designing and deploying AI agents, with available resources including a GitHub template, workshop materials, and practical setup guidance. The technique focuses on structured agent development patterns.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents that self-improve through autonomous loops. Covers use cases in ads optimization and SEO, and discusses tools like Loopany, memory systems, and tool integration to enable these capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses /goal feature and autonomous coding capabilities, comparing potential of new systems to Ralph-loop 2.0. He covers goal-setting mechanisms, mission exploration for long-horizon tasks, and showcases early-access tools like Crewlet and Superdesign for autonomous coding workflows.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Educational video overview of how Transformers and GPT work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. Discusses how GPT powers models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code provides a 10-minute explanation of Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model, covering tokenization, embeddings, and training mechanics. The video introduces core concepts like softmax, cross entropy loss, and optimizers (AdamW) as building blocks toward understanding GPT and attention mechanisms.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3, a series of open language models in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture, Mixture of Experts in latent space, and multi-token prediction in a novel way.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax releases M3, a language model incorporating MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache that reduces bottlenecks in both prefill and decode stages during inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a 7-step system design framework prioritizing problem understanding before technology selection. The framework covers requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints, using a URL shortener as the example interview problem.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling enables AI agents to request external tools, allowing frameworks to execute actions and loop back with results. This breaks agents free from stale training data and context limits by connecting them to real-time systems and files. The system prompt guides when the agent invokes each tool.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with tools, documentation, and guardrails for AWS service integration. It provides up-to-date AWS knowledge and best practices to improve agent success rates and reduce inefficiency.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers tutorial on running Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock with data residency and compliance guarantees. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and guardrails for governance.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers practice vibe coding, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code approaches. The video examines coding culture at Google across 2024-2026, including commentary on code generation practices and engineer workflows.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool that the creator argues is ending or disrupting open source. The video covers how Malus operates and its implications for open source maintainers, presented as a critical take on the company's role in the ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats depend on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that the best AI products generate customer loyalty and that fears of AI-driven SaaS collapse are exaggerated.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics, but the real challenge is not achieving intelligence. She critiques the humanoid robot race as myopic and contends that designing for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, should shape the billions of robots entering society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy enterprise giants by building a full integrated platform from day one rather than a single feature. She shares insights on enterprise sales strategy, the importance of customer social proof, and hiring philosophy as a key competitive advantage.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers and formal interviews are poor signals of real product-market fit. True PMF manifests as customer pull (cost inquiries) rather than push (keep-me-posted behavior), and requires deep embedded understanding of customer work rather than point-in-time conversations. He advocates betting on where AI models are heading, not just current capabilities.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof warns that agentic AI systems pose underappreciated security threats including prompt injection, unauthorized data deletion, and malicious actions. Businesses remain hesitant to deploy agentic AI despite its potential due to these overlooked risks.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's "This Is Tech Year" podcast episode features discussion of Anthropic's latest announcements with guest Joao, combining tech news coverage with local event commentary from Lisbon.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube shorts video comparing productivity dynamics between small agile teams and large teams. The content argues that small teams are more efficient at task completion while large teams suffer from excessive meetings and coordination overhead.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses surging demand for high bandwidth memory driven by the AI boom. Micron and SK Hynix are highlighted as key players shaping the future of memory infrastructure for AI workloads.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
